name: Dyte API
what_it_does: Dyte REST API — highly customizable real-time video and audio meeting infrastructure with programmable UI components, enabling agents to create meetings, manage participants, control recording, and build branded video experiences.
use_cases: Agents provisioning Dyte meeting rooms for on-demand video sessions with custom branding configurations
Telehealth platforms — agents creating HIPAA-compliant Dyte video sessions for patient-provider consultations
EdTech workflows — agents setting up Dyte classrooms with recording, whiteboard, and polling features for online education
Recording management — agents starting/stopping Dyte cloud recordings and retrieving media after session completion
Participant control — agents managing meeting participants (mute, kick, promote) via Dyte server-side API during live sessions
not_for: Simple video calls — use Daily.co for quick embeds without full platform customization overhead
High-scale broadcast to 10K+ viewers — use Mux or Agora for large audience live streaming
Audio-only podcasts — use Riverside or Zencastr for audio-optimized recording without video infrastructure
best_when: You need a customizable branded video experience where agents control meeting state server-side and UI components can be composed programmatically.
avoid_when: You need a simple drop-in video widget — Daily.co or Whereby are easier to integrate without custom UI composition.

auth.notes: API key (Organization ID + API key) via Basic auth or header. Keys from Dyte developer portal. Account-wide access. Participant auth tokens (short-lived JWT) generated server-side for client SDK access.

pricing.paid_tiers: Growth: pay-as-you-go from $0.004/min; Scale and Enterprise with volume discounts
pricing.notes: Generous free tier for development. Recording storage is separate from minutes. Enterprise plans include SLA and dedicated support.

performance.rate_limits: API: 500 req/min; meeting creation rate-limited per org; recording operations are async

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Well-documented API with OpenAPI spec. Error responses include code and message fields. Participant auth token generation is clearly documented. Webhook payloads include full session context.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Meeting creation is not idempotent — each call creates a new meeting. Meeting operations by meeting ID are idempotent. Participant token generation creates new tokens each time (not idempotent but safe).
known_agent_gotchas: Participant auth tokens expire (24h) — agents must generate fresh tokens for each new participant session
Meeting IDs and participant IDs are separate — both required for participant control operations
Recording compositing (custom layouts) requires preset configuration — default is grid layout
Dyte's UI Kit is framework-specific — React components differ from vanilla JS; agents must specify integration type
Active speaker and media events only available via client SDK — server-side API cannot subscribe to live media events

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II. HIPAA BAA available. GDPR compliant. Multi-region data residency. End-to-end encryption available. API key with org-wide access — no scope granularity. Participant tokens are short-lived JWTs.
